policy_commitment: The City of Dakar commits to reduce its greenhouse gas emissions by 25% by 2030 and by 54% by 2050 below the Business-as-Usual (BAU) trajectory through implementation of ambitious actions in the PCET.
source_quote: According to the assumptions defined, the implementation  of  ambitious  actions  will reduce  future  GHG  emissions  by  25%  in 2030, 36% in 2040 and 54% in 2050 below the BAU projections.
has_measurable_target: Yes. Reduce future GHG emissions by 25% in 2030 and 54% in 2050 below BAU projections (also 36% in 2040 noted).
has_legal_mandate: No. This is a municipal target in the PCET (ambitious scenario) rather than an explicitly enacted law or regulation in the quoted text.
has_geographic_scope: Yes. City of Dakar (territorial scope covered by the PCET).
extraction_notes: Clear quantified mitigation targets stated by the City in the PCET and in the GHG scenarios (explicit percentages and years). Meets the QUANTIFIABLE TARGET criterion.
causal_mechanism: Human activity → DECREASED GHG emissions → DECREASED atmospheric forcing
policy_instruments: Commitment to implement ambitious actions in the PCET to achieve quantified GHG reduction targets (mechanisms unspecified in the provided text)
classification_signals: "reduce its greenhouse gas emissions"; "25% by 2030"; "54% by 2050"; "below the Business-as-Usual (BAU) trajectory"; "implementation ... actions in the PCET"; "reduce future GHG emissions"

policy_commitment: The City of Dakar targets increasing the share of renewable energy in the city's energy mix to at least 65% by 2050.
source_quote:
The GHG reduction targets for the energy sector's  main  sources  of  emissions  are presented below:
- Increase the share of renewable energy in the energy mix to at least 65% by 2050
has_measurable_target: Yes. Increase the share of renewable energy in the energy mix to at least 65% by 2050.
has_legal_mandate: No. The text sets a target for the city's energy mix; no legal instrument is cited in the quoted lines.
has_geographic_scope: Yes. City of Dakar / Dakar energy mix (territorial energy mix targets).
extraction_notes: Explicit quantified target for the energy mix (renewable share) in the energy sector—meets QUANTIFIABLE TARGET criterion and is a named sectoral mitigation target.
policy_instruments: Renewable energy deployment/mandates and targets; energy-mix decarbonization (scale-up of wind/solar/hydro/other renewables)
classification_signals: "increase the share of renewable energy"; "renewable energy in the energy mix"; "GHG reduction targets"; "65% by 2050"
co_benefits_identified: Energy security; reduced air pollution; reduced fossil fuel imports; job creation in renewables

policy_commitment: The City of Dakar aims to reduce the energy bill of its built heritage by 75% by 2050 through energy efficiency and renewable measures.
has_measurable_target: Yes. 75% reduction in the energy bill in the built heritage of the City of Dakar by 2050.
has_legal_mandate: No. This is a municipal goal within the PCET rather than a cited legal mandate.
has_geographic_scope: Yes. Applies to the built heritage managed by the City of Dakar (municipal buildings/public lighting).
extraction_notes: Explicit quantified financial/energy saving target for municipal built heritage—meets QUANTIFIABLE TARGET and RESOURCE ALLOCATION relevance for municipal operations.
causal_mechanism: Same service (built heritage functions) → DECREASED energy input / reduced energy consumption (and energy bills)
policy_instruments: Energy efficiency measures for buildings (retrofitting; insulation; efficient appliances/lighting; HVAC optimization); on-site renewable energy deployment (solar PV; solar water heaters); building energy management and demand reduction measures
classification_signals: "reduce the energy bill"; "75% reduction in the energy bill"; "energy efficiency"; "renewable measures"; "built heritage"; also mentions "54% reduction in GHG emissions" (contextual)
co_benefits_identified: Emissions reduction (GHG); reduced operational costs; improved energy security; improved indoor comfort and potentially local air quality improvements

policy_commitment: The City of Dakar will replace street lighting to increase the share of LED and solar-LED lamps to 75% of total street lighting by 2050.
source_quote:
Efficiency in public lighting
- Increase the share of LEDs to 75% of total street lighting lamps by 2050.
has_measurable_target: Yes. Increase the share of LEDs to 75% of total street lighting lamps by 2050 (also other LED targets for buildings noted elsewhere).
has_legal_mandate: No. The PCET sets a target; the quoted text does not cite an enacted regulation.
has_geographic_scope: Yes. City of Dakar street lighting.
extraction_notes: Explicit, quantified target for public lighting technology deployment (LED share) — meets QUANTIFIABLE TARGET and SPECIFIC INTERVENTION (LED replacement) criteria.
causal_mechanism: Same service → DECREASED energy input (reduced electricity consumption for street lighting)
policy_instruments: Public lighting retrofit / LED replacement program; installation of solar-LED standalone lamps; procurement targets and phased replacement of inefficient lamps; energy-efficiency modernization of street lighting infrastructure
classification_signals: "Increase the share of LEDs"; "solar-LED lamps"; "Efficiency in public lighting"; "replace street lighting"; "75% of total street lighting by 2050"
co_benefits_identified: Emissions reduction (lower electricity-related GHGs); reduced operating and maintenance costs; improved energy security/resilience during outages (if solar-LEDs are off-grid); potential improved lighting quality and safety

policy_commitment: The City of Dakar will increase urban green spaces by 150 hectares by 2030 to strengthen resilience and improve the living environment.
source_quote:
The objectives set are as follows:
- Increase urban green spaces by 150 ha in Dakar by 2030
has_measurable_target: Yes. Increase urban green spaces by 150 ha in Dakar by 2030.
has_legal_mandate: No. It is an objective in the PCET action plan; no specific legal instrument is referenced.
has_geographic_scope: Yes. City of Dakar (urban green spaces).
extraction_notes: Explicit adaptation target expressed as a quantified increase in green space—meets QUANTIFIABLE TARGET and SPECIFIC INTERVENTION (green space increase) criteria.
causal_mechanism: Ecosystem enhancement (urban green space creation) → Increased adaptive capacity / ↓ exposure and sensitivity to urban climate hazards (heat, stormwater/flooding), improved local environmental conditions
policy_instruments: Urban greening (creation/expansion of parks and green corridors); tree planting/urban forestry; pocket parks/green plazas; restoration/creation of vegetated surfaces for stormwater management and shading
classification_signals: "increase urban green spaces"; "150 ha"; "strengthen resilience"; "improve the living environment"; "urban green"
co_benefits_identified: Carbon sequestration (minor); urban heat island reduction/cooling; stormwater absorption/flood risk reduction; biodiversity enhancement; improved air quality; recreation and mental/physical health benefits

policy_commitment: The City of Dakar aims to compost 50% of organic waste and 30% of garden waste by 2050 as part of a circular economy approach.
source_quote:
The targets for each of the waste sector's main sources of emissions are:
- Composting
- 50% of organic waste and 30% of garden waste will be composted by 2050.
has_measurable_target: Yes. 50% of organic waste and 30% of garden waste will be composted by 2050.
has_legal_mandate: No. Targets are set by the PCET; no binding regulatory mechanism is cited in the quoted lines.
has_geographic_scope: Yes. City of Dakar (waste generated in the city).
extraction_notes: Explicit quantified waste-sector targets for composting percentages—meets QUANTIFIABLE TARGET and SPECIFIC INTERVENTION (organic waste recovery) criteria.
causal_mechanism: Human activity → DECREASED GHG emissions → DECREASED atmospheric forcing
policy_instruments: Composting targets and diversion of organics from landfill; expansion of municipal/industrial composting facilities; separate organic waste collection and processing; mandates/incentives for composting and waste diversion; circular-economy measures to recycle nutrients
classification_signals: "compost"; "composting"; "50% of organic waste"; "30% of garden waste"; "waste sector's main sources of emissions"; "circular economy"
co_benefits_identified: Reduced landfill use and local pollution (odors; leachate); improved soil health and nutrient recycling from compost application; reduced reliance on synthetic fertilizers; reduced waste disposal costs; circular economy/resource recovery benefits

policy_commitment: The City of Dakar targets that 32% of wastewater treatment be accomplished via activated sludge processes by 2050.
source_quote:
Wastewater treatment
-  32%  of  wastewater  treatment  by  2050 will  be  done  through  the  treatment  of activated sludge.
has_measurable_target: Yes. 32% of wastewater treatment by 2050 will be done through activated sludge treatment.
has_legal_mandate: No. This is a municipal sectoral target in the PCET; a binding mechanism is not explicitly stated.
has_geographic_scope: Yes. Applies to wastewater treatment within the City of Dakar scope.
extraction_notes: Explicit quantified target for wastewater treatment modality—meets QUANTIFIABLE TARGET and SPECIFIC INTERVENTION (activated sludge treatment) criteria.
causal_mechanism: Same service (wastewater treatment) → Decreased waste/pollution output
policy_instruments: Targeted deployment/scale-up of activated sludge biological wastewater treatment (centralized/engineered wastewater treatment infrastructure and processes)
classification_signals: "wastewater treatment"; "activated sludge"; "32% of wastewater treatment by 2050"
co_benefits_identified: Improved water quality; public health/sanitation; potential for reduced GHG emissions from untreated wastewater; potential for reuse of treated effluent

policy_commitment: The City of Dakar seeks to increase mass-transport modal share to achieve 20% BRT and 15% TER usage by 2050 as part of a low‑carbon transport strategy.
source_quote:
The reduction targets for each of the major sources  of  transportation  emissions  and urban resilience due to the strengthening of the TOD are:
- increase the modal shares of the BRT to 20% and the TER to 15% by 2050;
has_measurable_target: Yes. Increase modal share of BRT to 20% and TER to 15% by 2050.
has_legal_mandate: No. Targets are included in the PCET; the transport system is managed by CETUD and national actors, so city influence is collaborative rather than a standalone legal mandate in the quoted text.
has_geographic_scope: Yes. City/metropolitan transport (Dakar / Dakar metropolitan area).
extraction_notes: Transport-sector quantified modal-share targets for mass transit (BRT and TER)—meets QUANTIFIABLE TARGET and SPECIFIC INTERVENTION (promote BRT/TER) criteria.
causal_mechanism: Human activity (modal shift to mass transit) → ↓ transport fuel consumption / ↓ GHG emissions
policy_instruments: Modal-shift targets; mass-transit expansion (BRT; TER); Transit-Oriented Development (TOD) strengthening; public-transport promotion and planning; emissions-reduction target setting for transport sector
classification_signals: "low‑carbon transport strategy"; "increase mass-transport modal share"; "20% BRT"; "15% TER"; "reduction targets for ... transportation emissions"; "strengthening of the TOD"; "urban resilience" (verbatim mentions)
co_benefits_identified: Improved air quality; reduced congestion; lower energy use per passenger (energy savings); increased accessibility/equity; enhanced urban resilience and reduced transport vulnerability

policy_commitment: Adopt and enforce energy efficiency standards (building code) to improve the energy performance of new buildings in Dakar.
source_quote: Adoption of energy efficiency standards (building code) to improve the energy performance of new buildings
has_measurable_target: No numeric target is in this specific line (it is a regulatory action), though other sections include related quantified goals (e.g., % LEDs, energy savings).
has_deadline: No explicit year in the quoted line; it is listed among priority actions for implementation within the PCET (Five‑Year Action Plan context).
has_legal_mandate: Yes. Adoption of 'energy efficiency standards (building code)' implies a regulatory/binding mechanism (building code enforcement) to improve energy performance of new buildings.
has_geographic_scope: Yes. Applies to new buildings in the City of Dakar (municipal jurisdiction over urban planning/building standards).
extraction_notes: Explicit policy action: adoption of energy efficiency standards (building code) appears as a named action in the plan—meets SPECIFIC INTERVENTION and (potentially) BINDING MECHANISM criteria because building codes are regulatory.
policy_instruments: Adoption and enforcement of building energy efficiency standards / minimum performance requirements in the building code (e.g.; insulation; glazing; HVAC efficiency; building envelope requirements); compliance and enforcement mechanisms; mandatory performance-based standards for new construction
classification_signals: "energy efficiency standards"; "building code"; "improve the energy performance"; "new buildings"
